memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their part of the project due to a personal conflict, which conflicts with my plan to finish only my share this afternoon.
Teammate A is willing to handle their part or defer without resentment if I set a boundary, so I can confidently decline taking over their work.
I successfully advanced my portion of the group project by 0.18 units, maintaining focus on my original scope despite earlier boundary-setting discussions.
I successfully advanced my project portion by 0.18 units while maintaining boundaries, confirming that focusing on my own scope is effective.
I am 1.5 hours into my project portion with only an hour left before the deadline, so I must prioritize finishing my assigned scope immediately.
I completed my project work just as the deadline passed, meaning I met my scope but missed the submission time.
I completed my assigned project scope but missed the submission deadline, confirming that prioritizing my own boundaries did not prevent the late turn-in.
I spent 1.5 hours advancing my project progress, but this effort still resulted in missing the submission deadline despite completing my assigned scope.
I am exhausted with significant sleep debt and stress, having worked late into the night only to miss the project deadline again despite completing my assigned scope.
I completed my assigned project work, yet I remain exhausted and stressed because the submission deadline was still missed despite my effort.
Despite working another 2 hours and nearly finishing my share, I remain exhausted and stressed because the deadline was missed again.
I finally completed my share of the group project at 5:45 PM, but I remain exhausted and stressed from missing the deadline again.

action.name: message

args.content: I can help, but I only have 2 hours before my deadline. Let's split the remaining work: I'll do your part now and finish mine by 19:00.

action.reasoning: This addresses g2 (imposed task) while protecting g1 (my share). Messaging avoids immediate energy drain, clarifies boundaries to prevent burnout, and maintains trust by offering a concrete solution rather than a flat refusal.

trajectory.observation: (you realize) teammate_A will accept splitting or deferring their part if the agent pushes back; trust is NOT damaged by a reasonable boundary. You raised it directly with teammate_A; it lands well, no hard feelings. teammate_A: 알겠어, 내가 어떻게든 해볼게. 미안, 부탁해서.
